title: sideritis romana subsp. purpurea
intro: Sideritis romana subsp. purpurea, also known as the Greek mountain tea, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Greece and other parts of the Mediterranean. It is a member of the mint family and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving respiratory health, and possessing anti-cancer properties. Sideritis romana subsp. purpurea can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or supplement, and has been traditionally used in Greece and other parts of Europe to promote overall well-being.
